Class Path

java.lang.Object
org.firstinspires.ftc.teamcode.util.Path
public class Path extends Object
Path is a class that organizes Waypoint objects into a single easy to manage object that will give suggestions for drive variables and gives access to information such as where the bot currently is on its mission.
Author:
YesItIsEvan

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    static enum 
    Path.Ahead
    Speed modes for the robot that are referred to in the same way as the speed modes for the cyclops in Subnautica.
    static enum 
    Path.traceMode
    Modes for how the bot will follow the waypoints

    Gives a suggested drive power after considering the distance to the waypoint, drive power, and speed mode

    If the bot is set to STOP_WHEN_COMPLETE then this condition will turn false once the bot has reached its final waypoint

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Path

      public Path(double currentRunTime_s, com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.HardwareMap hardwareMap)
      Forms a new path for you to forge. Use the Path.addWaypoint(Waypoint) method to add new points along the path.
      Parameters:
      currentRunTime_s - The current runtime of the Op-mode
      hardwareMap - Hardware map for the voltage sensor to give you better drive suggestions

    • Path

      public Path(org.firstinspires.ftc.robotcore.external.navigation.Pose2D current, double currentRunTime_s, com.qualcomm.robotcore.hardware.HardwareMap hardwareMap)
      Forms a new path for you to forge. Use the Path.addWaypoint(Waypoint) method to add new points along the path.
      Parameters:
      current - The current Pose2D of the bot for presetting off of (0,0)
      currentRunTime_s - The current runtime of the Op-mode
      hardwareMap - Hardware map for the voltage sensor to give you better drive suggestions

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update(org.firstinspires.ftc.robotcore.external.navigation.Pose2D robotPos, double currentRunTime)
      Updates Path with the current robot position to determine if the robot has reached the waypoint and can proceed to the next. This also updates the suggested drive parameters necessary for reaching the waypoint.
      Parameters:
      robotPos - The current position of the robot
      currentRunTime - The current runtime in seconds

    • addWaypoint

      public void addWaypoint(Waypoint newWaypoint)
      Appends a new waypoint at the end of the path

      Note: Use during initialization

      Parameters:
      newWaypoint - A new instance of the Waypoint class for Path to organize alongside the current waypoints.

    • setTraceMode

      public void setTraceMode(Path.traceMode followMode)
      Sets how the bot will follow the path. Round loops will have the bot go back to the first waypoint once it has reached the last waypoint, meanwhile reversing loops will have the bot reverse the order in which it follows the path.
      Parameters:
      followMode - The trace mode defined by the Path.traceMode enum

    • setTolerance

      public void setTolerance(double cm, double degrees)
      Sets the waypoint tolerance of the bot when getting to a target waypoint. If the bot is within these parameters away from a target waypoint then the bot will proceed to the next waypoint. This prevents the bot from getting stuck trying to achieve an exact position.

      Default: 5 cm, 1 degree

      Parameters:
      cm - Coordinate tolerance in centimeters
      degrees - Rotational tolerance in degrees

    • setDriveTune

      public void setDriveTune(double drive, double strafe, double turn)
      Sets tuning coefficients for suggested drive. Tuning coefficients make sure that the amount of influence that each drive component has respect on another. This makes the bot drive in predictable and straight lines towards its waypoint.

      Suggestion: This method is not really needed as whenever changing speed modes, the tuning variables already get changed accordingly, so do not use this method unless absolutely necessary

      Parameters:
      drive - Drive (forward and backward) tuning coefficient
      strafe - Strafe (Crab left and right) tuning coefficient
      turn - Turn (Rotate left and right) tuning coefficient

    • setSpeedMode

      public void setSpeedMode(Path.Ahead ahead)
      Sets the speed mode of the bot and will adjust the drive tuning accordingly. This also impacts which function Path will use to determine drive power so it is suggested to use this method instead of Path.setPower().

      Parameters:
      ahead - The speed mode defined by the Path.Ahead enum

    • setPower

      public void setPower(double power)
      Will set the coefficient that determines the bots overall drive power.

      NOT TO BE USED TO SET OVERALL SPEED!

      Parameters:
      power - The power multiplier between (0,1)

    • getSuggestedDrive

      public double getSuggestedDrive()
      Gives a suggested drive power after considering the distance to the waypoint, drive power, and speed mode
      Returns:
      Suggested drive power

    • getSuggestedStrafe

      public double getSuggestedStrafe()
      Gives a suggested strafe power after considering the distance to the waypoint, drive power, and speed mode
      Returns:
      Suggested strafe power

    • getSuggestedTurn

      public double getSuggestedTurn()
      Gives a suggested turn power after considering the distance to the waypoint, drive power, and speed mode
      Returns:
      Suggested turning power

    • getCurrentWaypoint

      public int getCurrentWaypoint()
      Gives an integer value representing the index of the waypoint that the bot is currently targeting.
      Returns:
      The index of the bot's current waypoint inside the waypoint array that Path stores.

    • isRunning

      public boolean isRunning()
      If the bot is set to STOP_WHEN_COMPLETE then this condition will turn false once the bot has reached its final waypoint
      Returns:
      whether the bot is still running the mission
